title: Tenant Signup Ceremony with WebAuthn Enrollment

story.as_a: human operator establishing a new loopctl tenant
story.i_want_to: enroll a hardware authenticator (YubiKey, Touch ID, Windows Hello, or any FIDO2 device) as the root of trust for my tenant during signup
story.so_that: the tenant's entire chain of custody traces back to a physical touch only I can perform, and no agent in the system can ever impersonate my root authority

rationale: Every tenant in loopctl needs a human root of trust that agents cannot forge. WebAuthn is the only widely-deployed standard that binds a signing operation to a physical device hold, and FIDO2 hardware authenticators (or platform authenticators like Touch ID / Windows Hello) are unforgeable by software. By requiring enrollment at tenant creation time and making it the gate for all subsequent destructive operations, we establish a Layer 0 anchor (per the chain-of-custody v2 spec) that the rest of the trust model hangs from. Without this ceremony, every other invariant in Chain of Custody v2 degrades to policy-level enforcement that a creative adversary can reach around.

acceptance_criteria.description: A new LiveView at `/signup` collects tenant metadata (name, slug, contact_email) and initiates a WebAuthn registration ceremony via the browser's `navigator.credentials.create()` API. The ceremony uses platform-agnostic relying party ID `loopctl.com` and accepts both cross-platform (YubiKey, etc) and platform (Touch ID, Windows Hello) authenticators.

Migration adds a `tenants.root_authenticators` table with columns: id (uuid PK), tenant_id (uuid FK), credential_id (bytea, the FIDO2 credential ID), public_key (bytea, COSE-encoded), attestation_format (string), sign_count (integer), friendly_name (string), inserted_at, last_used_at. Unique index on (tenant_id, credential_id). One-to-many relationship supports multiple backup authenticators per tenant.

Server-side WebAuthn verification uses a dedicated Elixir library (Wax or equivalent) to verify the FIDO2 attestation statement. The public key, attestation format, and sign counter are extracted and stored in `root_authenticators`. Invalid attestations return 422 with `error.reason: invalid_attestation` and a link to `https://loopctl.com/wiki/tenant-signup`.

A tenant record is created in the database ONLY after the WebAuthn enrollment succeeds. The `tenants.status` field is `:pending_enrollment` until enrollment completes, then `:active`. A tenant stuck in `:pending_enrollment` for more than 15 minutes is cleaned up by an Oban job.

Duplicate slug or email returns 422 with a stable error code (`slug_taken` or `email_taken`) and a remediation URL. Slugs are normalized to lowercase kebab-case with a length limit of 64 characters and a regex of `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]$`.

The signup flow supports multiple authenticator types in a single ceremony: the operator can enroll a primary YubiKey AND a backup platform authenticator in the same session, both recorded in `root_authenticators`. Minimum one authenticator is required to complete signup; up to 5 can be enrolled in the initial ceremony.

Tenant signup is the ONLY path to create a tenant. There is no admin endpoint to create tenants without WebAuthn, no password fallback, no email magic link, no OAuth, no invitation token. Attempting to create a tenant via any other path returns 403 or 404 as appropriate.

The signup LiveView follows `docs/design-system.md`: dark mode, Geist typography, slate palette, cool blue-gray accent, rounded-md cards, no rounded-xl, no gradients, no warm grays. Error states use hand-written Tailwind (no daisyUI). The hardware-key prompt uses Phoenix.Component `<.icon name="hero-key" />` styling.

After successful enrollment, the operator is redirected to `/tenants/:id/onboarding` (a subsequent LiveView) that guides them through the next steps: audit key generation (US-26.0.2), system article tour, first project creation, first agent registration. Each step is marked complete as the operator performs it.

OpenAPI spec updates: new schemas for `TenantSignupRequest`, `WebAuthnChallenge`, `WebAuthnAttestation`, `TenantResponse`. New operation specs on the signup LiveView-backed routes. Swagger UI at `/api/docs` reflects the new schemas.

A system-scoped wiki article at slug `tenant-signup` is created (content authored in US-26.0.5) and the signup LiveView includes a `learn_more` link pointing at it. The article slug is reserved in this story even if the full content ships in US-26.0.5.

CHANGELOG entry added under an `[Unreleased]` section describing the new signup flow, the WebAuthn requirement, and the deprecation of any previous tenant creation path. CLAUDE.md's Security & Trust Model section gains a brief note pointing at `docs/chain-of-custody-v2.md` as the spec.

test_cases.name: Successful tenant signup with a FIDO2 attestation creates tenant, authenticator record, and active status
preconditions: Stub WebAuthn library returns a valid attestation for a test credential
steps: POST /signup with name, slug, email, and a stub WebAuthn attestation response
Query tenants table and root_authenticators table
expected_results: Response is 200 with the new tenant ID and redirect to /tenants/:id/onboarding
tenants row exists with status = active
root_authenticators row exists with the correct credential_id and public_key
tenants.inserted_at is within the last 60 seconds

test_cases.name: Invalid WebAuthn attestation rejects signup and creates no tenant record
Duplicate slug returns 422 with stable error code
Multiple authenticators can be enrolled in a single signup ceremony
preconditions: Stub returns valid attestations for two distinct credentials
steps: POST /signup with two attestation responses in sequence
expected_results: root_authenticators has 2 rows, both tied to the new tenant_id

test_cases.name: Pending enrollment tenant is cleaned up after 15 minutes
preconditions: tenant = fixture(:tenant, status: :pending_enrollment, inserted_at: 16 minutes ago)
expected_results: Any associated root_authenticators rows (there should be none) are also gone

test_cases.name: Tenant creation via any path other than /signup is rejected
Signup LiveView renders with correct design-system classes
expected_results: Response HTML includes the expected form elements
CSS classes include slate-*, rounded-md, and Geist font references
No daisyUI classes (card, btn-primary, alert) in the output

technical_notes: WebAuthn library: use Wax (https://github.com/tanguilp/wax) or equivalent FIDO2-verified Elixir package. Verify attestation type and format at enrollment time.
Relying party ID (rp_id): `loopctl.com` for production. `localhost` for dev. Configure per-env in config/*.exs.
Credential public key format: COSE-encoded, stored as bytea. Verification on subsequent authentications uses the same COSE key.
Sign count: stored for clone detection. Every subsequent WebAuthn assertion verification must check the sign count increases (or stays 0 for authenticators that don't implement it).
LiveView file: lib/loopctl_web/live/signup_live.ex — route configured in the existing public live_session with on_mount fetch_current_scope.
Client-side JS: a small hook in assets/js/hooks/webauthn.js wraps navigator.credentials.create() and posts the response back via LiveView push_event.
The signup form fields are built with Phoenix.Component.to_form/2 and <.input> from core_components.
Testing WebAuthn: use Wax.test_helpers or mock the library at the behaviour level per loopctl's DI convention (config/test.exs sets :loopctl, :webauthn_adapter).
Pending enrollment cleanup worker: lib/loopctl/workers/pending_enrollment_cleanup_worker.ex with Oban Cron schedule {"*/5 * * * *", PendingEnrollmentCleanupWorker}.
The /signup route is added to the public live_session (the :current_user or :guest scope depending on existing naming).
Tenant creation must remain atomic: the tenant, the root_authenticators row, and the initial audit_chain genesis entry all in a single Ecto.Multi.
Reference: docs/chain-of-custody-v2.md section 9 for the full signup ceremony spec.
